// BAD: Vec<Box<T>> causes pointer chasing and poor cache locality
struct ParticleSystem {
    particles: Vec<Box<Particle>>,
}

struct Particle {
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    z: f32,
    vx: f32,
    vy: f32,
    vz: f32,
    life: f32,
}

impl ParticleSystem {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let particles = (0..10_000)
            .map(|i| Box::new(Particle {
                x: 0.0, y: 0.0, z: 0.0,
                vx: 0.0, vy: 0.0, vz: 0.0,
                life: 1.0,
            }))
            .collect();
        Self { particles }
    }

    fn tick(&mut self, dt: f32) {
        for p in &mut self.particles {
            p.x += p.vx * dt;
            p.y += p.vy * dt;
            p.z += p.vz * dt;
            p.life -= dt * 0.01;
        }
    }
}
